sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते।
एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम्॥
transliteration:
indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣṭhānam ucyate |
etair vimohayaty eṣa jñānam āvṛtya dehinam ||
literal_meaning: The senses, mind and intellect are said to be its (lust’s) seat; through these it clouds knowledge and bewilders the embodied being.
contextual_meaning: Desire hijacks the very instruments meant for self-regulation—senses, mind, intellect—turning them into Trojan horses that smother discriminative awareness and replace it with compulsive storylines.
psychological_state: Executive functions colonized by addictive impulse; metacognitive blindness.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media past bedtime, each swipe feels autonomous yet you can’t stop even though the alarm for work is set for six.
anchor_text: Notice when desire hijacks your senses, mind, and intellect—pause and reclaim clarity.
